vulnerability.reference: Invalid molecules that still score well on property predictors mislead optimization algorithms, creating false feedback loops.
vulnerability.generated: Scientists' reliance on generated molecular outputs without rapid structural validation introduces susceptibility to propagating chemically invalid hits.

impact.reference: Property-conditioned screens overestimate molecule quality, causing costly pursuit of false hits.
impact.generated: Wasted screening assays and resources on nonviable molecules, delaying lead identification and increasing discovery costs.

dose_response.reference: Non-autoregressive discrete diffusion with fragment remasking generates invalid structures, so when drug discovery scientists use property-conditioned generation without sanity checks, overestimated molecule quality leads to costly pursuit of false hits.
dose_response.generated: Non-autoregressive discrete diffusion with fragment remasking generating chemically invalid structures, through property-conditioned generation by drug discovery scientists, triggers reliance on unvalidated outputs, leading to wasted screening assays, delayed lead identification, and increased discovery costs.
